events.title: Tanker truck spill releases about 2,500 gallons of diesel into Gorman Creek near Pyramid Lake, California
events.description: A tanker truck traveling on Interstate 5 reportedly suffered a tire blowout that damaged valves, releasing about 2,500 gallons of diesel fuel into Gorman Creek near the Gorman area. Responders reportedly built a dike and conducted cleanup to limit impacts toward Pyramid Lake.

location.name: Gorman Creek near Pyramid Lake (Gorman area), California, USA

sources.headline: Tanker truck spill releases ~2,500 gallons of diesel fuel into Gorman Creek (near Pyramid Lake), California, USA

events.title: Nitrogen release creates oxygen-deficient atmosphere at Union Carbide facility in Hahnville, Louisiana
events.description: A U.S. Chemical Safety Board incident report describes a nitrogen purging operation that created an oxygen-deficient atmosphere in a temporary enclosure at a Union Carbide-related facility in Hahnville, Louisiana. The incident is treated here as a hazardous gas release leading to an asphyxiation emergency within an industrial setting.

location.name: Union Carbide facility, Hahnville, Louisiana, USA

sources.source_name: U.S. Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board (CSB)
sources.headline: Union Carbide nitrogen release causes oxygen-deficient atmosphere (industrial gas incident), Hahnville, Louisiana, USA

organizations: U.S. Chemical Safety and Hazard Investigation Board (CSB)

events.title: Northern Black Hills blizzard peaks with record snow depth in Lead, South Dakota
events.description: A multi-day blizzard that began in late February continued into early March in the Northern Black Hills. On March 1, Lead, South Dakota recorded extreme snowfall and a record measured snow depth, according to NWS documentation.

sources.headline: Lead Blizzard 1998

key_quotes.quote: Lead recorded 32.4 inches of snowfall that day... deepest measured snow depth of 73 inches.

events.title: Tropical Cyclone Nathan persists in the Coral Sea (Australia region)
events.description: Tropical Cyclone Nathan was tracked in the Coral Sea during March 1998. The Australian Bureau of Meteorology describes it as a weak system that persisted for several days while moving generally southeast off far North Queensland before weakening.

location.name: Canberra, Australia (proxy for Australia region cyclone basin)

sources.headline: Tropical Cyclone Nathan

key_quotes.quote: A weak tropical cyclone... persisted in the Coral Sea from March 20–26, 1998.

events.title: Severe thunderstorms produce reported tornado and large hail across NE South Carolina and SE North Carolina
events.description: Severe thunderstorms affected parts of northeast South Carolina and southeast North Carolina on March 20, with reports including a tornado in Robeson County along with large hail, wind damage, and heavy rainfall estimates.

location.name: Raleigh, North Carolina, USA (proxy for SE North Carolina/NE South Carolina event area)

sources.headline: 1998-Mar-20 severe

key_quotes.quote: ...producing a reported tornado in Robeson County, large hail, wind damage, and heavy rain.

events.title: Mw 8.1 Balleny Islands region earthquake (Southern Ocean/Antarctica)
events.description: A great (Mw 8.1) intraplate earthquake occurred in the Balleny Islands region near Antarctica at 03:12:25 UTC on 25 March 1998. The USGS publication discusses the event and related aftershock/stress-triggering analysis.

location.name: Balleny Islands region (Southern Ocean, near Antarctica)

sources.headline: 1998-03-25 03:12:25 UTC 8.1 M earthquake
Did stress triggering cause large-fault aftershocks of the 25 March 1998 Mw8.1 Antarctic plate earthquake?

events.title: Mw 6.6 Fandoqa (Kerman Province) earthquake, Iran
events.description: A damaging Mw 6.6 earthquake struck the Fandoqa/Golbaf area in Kerman Province, Iran on 14 March 1998. The referenced paper analyzes aspects of the event; the draft notes fatalities, injuries, and village damage, but impacts are not directly verifiable from the provided snippet alone.

sources.headline: Geophysical Journal International article (Vol 146, Issue 2, p.371)

events.title: Mw 6.7 Auckland Islands offshore earthquake (New Zealand region)
events.description: A magnitude 6.7 earthquake occurred offshore near the Auckland Islands in the New Zealand region at 21:08:08 UTC on 20 March 1998. The provided source is a secondary compilation page and does not clearly establish the specific event details without additional primary confirmation.

location.name: Offshore Auckland Islands, New Zealand region

sources.headline: Biggest earthquakes in Auckland Islands, New Zealand (region)

key_quotes.quote: Biggest earthquakes in Auckland Islands, New Zealand (region)

events.title: Fires enter Sungai Wain Protection Forest during El Niño drought
events.description: In early March 1998, fires entered the Sungai Wain Protection Forest in East Kalimantan amid the 1997/98 El Niño drought. The source describes ignition linked to adjacent land uses and threats to a primary-forest reserve and wildlife refuge.

location.name: Sungai Wain Protection Forest (Hutan Lindung Sungai Wain), East Kalimantan, Indonesia

sources.headline: Sungai Wain Protection Forest (Hutan Lindung Sungai Wain) fire case study (1997/98 El Niño period)

key_quotes.quote: fires entered the Sungai Wain Protection Forest in early March 1998

events.title: Smoke from peat-swamp fires closes Pekanbaru airport for several hours
events.description: A news item archived by Asia-Pacific Solidarity reports that smoke from coastal peat-swamp fires in Riau province forced Pekanbaru airport to close for several hours. The report also noted concern about haze impacts if winds shifted.

sources.headline: Indonesia netnews archive item referencing Reuters report on smoke and fires

key_quotes.quote: smoke from fires in coastal peat swamps in Riau province forced Pekanbaru airport to close

events.title: Reported hotspots and fire spread in East Aceh toward Gunung Leuser area
events.description: An archived news roundup item reports hotspots in East Aceh with active fire spread described as moving toward the Gunung Leuser area. The report attributes causes to local land-clearing and illegal logging activities.

location.name: East Aceh, Aceh, Indonesia (toward Gunung Leuser area)

sources.headline: Indonesia netnews archive item referencing Kompas report on hotspots in East Aceh

key_quotes.quote: hotspots in East Aceh ... fire spreading toward Gunung Leuser

events.title: Renewed fires around Samarinda bring dense haze and reduced visibility
events.description: A Los Angeles Times report from March 1998 describes renewed, human-caused fires around Samarinda in East Kalimantan. The article notes haze conditions that reduced visibility and disrupted transport, with concerns about future haze later in the season.

sources.headline: Fires and haze around Samarinda (East Kalimantan)

key_quotes.quote: fires ... around Samarinda ... producing haze ... reducing visibility

events.title: Report relays ~50,000 hectares burned in Kutai National Park by end of March 1998
events.description: A web-posted item relays claims from an internal WWF report that fires ravaged Kutai National Park in East Kalimantan, with burned area reported as reaching about 50,000 hectares by the end of March 1998. The account describes major ecological impacts but is presented second-hand.

location.name: Kutai National Park, East Kalimantan, Indonesia

sources.headline: Kalimantan fires item relaying WWF report on Kutai National Park impacts

key_quotes.quote: burned area ... reaching about 50,000 hectares by the end of March 1998

events.title: Great Barrier Reef-wide aerial survey begins to assess 1998 mass coral bleaching
events.description: During the 1998 coral bleaching event on the Great Barrier Reef, a reef-wide aerial survey began on 9 March 1998 to assess bleaching extent and intensity across hundreds of reefs. The survey effort is described in research dataset records associated with the Great Barrier Reef coral bleaching event.

sources.headline: Coral bleaching events - Great Barrier Reef
GBR coral mass (AIMS dataset record)

events.title: Kyoto Protocol opened for signature at UN Headquarters
events.description: The Kyoto Protocol to the UNFCCC was opened for signature at United Nations Headquarters, beginning the treaty’s signature period. This advanced the international climate policy framework adopted at COP3 in Kyoto.

location.name: United Nations Headquarters, New York, United States

sources.headline: Kyoto Protocol Opens for Signature
Kyoto Protocol to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change

key_quotes.quote: opened for signature at UN Headquarters in New York on 16 March 1998

events.title: USFWS final ESA listing rule for seven freshwater mussels in eastern Gulf Slope drainages
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a final rule listing five freshwater mussel species as endangered and two as threatened from eastern Gulf Slope drainages. The rule established federal protections under the Endangered Species Act, with an effective date stated in the notice.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Final Rule To List the Purple Bankclimber Mussel, the Orange-nacre Mucket Mussel, and Five Other Freshwater Mussels as Endangered or Threatened

key_quotes.quote: Final rule... to list... freshwater mussels as endangered or threatened

events.title: EPA direct final action approving California CO maintenance plans and redesignations
events.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency published a direct final rule approving California State Implementation Plan maintenance plans and requests to redesignate multiple carbon monoxide planning areas to attainment. The notice described an effective date contingent on the absence of adverse comments.

sources.headline: Approval and Promulgation of Implementation Plans and Designation of Areas for Air Quality Planning Purposes; State of California; Carbon Monoxide

key_quotes.quote: direct final rule... approving maintenance plans... redesignate... carbon monoxide areas to attainment

events.title: NMFS proposed ESA listings for multiple West Coast Chinook salmon ESUs
events.description: NOAA’s National Marine Fisheries Service published a proposed rule under the Endangered Species Act addressing several Chinook salmon evolutionarily significant units (ESUs). The proposal included endangered and threatened listing proposals and described related actions such as ESU redefinitions and critical habitat considerations.

sources.headline: Endangered and Threatened Species: Proposed Endangered Status for Two Chinook Salmon ESUs and Threatened Status for Five Chinook Salmon ESUs; Proposed Rule

key_quotes.quote: Proposed endangered status for two Chinook salmon ESUs and threatened status for five Chinook salmon ESUs

events.title: USFWS proposed revisions to migratory game bird hunting baiting regulations
events.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service published a proposed rule to clarify and simplify migratory game bird hunting regulations related to baiting and baited areas. The proposal aimed to improve enforceability while addressing how certain habitat management practices are treated under the regulations.

sources.headline: Migratory Bird Hunting; Baiting and Baited Areas

key_quotes.quote: proposed rule... clarify and simplify... baiting and baited areas
